data.title: Buy Rating Backed by Pivotal Olvi-Vec Ovarian Cancer Trial Progress and Multi-Indication Upside Potential
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Jason McCarthy has maintained their bullish stance on GNLX stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the advancement of Olvi-Vec in a pivotal Phase 3 trial for platinum-resistant or refractory ovarian cancer, with top-line results expected in the second half of 2026. He notes that earlier Phase 2 data in this setting showed markedly stronger response rates and progression-free survival than historical benchmarks, and that regulatory alignment on an interim overall survival analysis could allow for full approval without an additional confirmatory study.

McCarthy also highlights encouraging interim results from systemic, intravenous Olvi-Vec programs in both non-small cell and small cell lung cancer, which provide additional optionality beyond the lead ovarian cancer indication. From a valuation standpoint, he models U.S. commercialization of Olvi-Vec in ovarian cancer in 2027 and in NSCLC in 2029, applies risk-adjusted revenues and a 20% discount rate, and arrives at a $12 twelve-month price target, which implies meaningful upside from current levels and supports maintaining a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, H.C. Wainwright also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $31.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: BUY Rating on SELLAS: Pivotal REGAL Phase 3 Progress, Expanding AML Pipeline, and Cash Runway into 2027 Support Upside Potential
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Jason McCarthy maintained a Buy rating on SELLAS Life Sciences Group yesterday and set a price target of $10.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to SELLAS’s clinical progress and financial position. He highlights that the pivotal Phase 3 REGAL trial of galinpepimut-S in second-remission AML is close to a decisive overall-survival readout, with interim data showing survival well above historical benchmarks and a slower-than-expected event accrual that may signal a favorable treatment effect.

In addition, he points to the start of the Phase 2 first-line AML study for SLS009 in combination with venetoclax and azacitidine, which is built on encouraging response and survival data in the relapsed/refractory setting and follows an FDA-endorsed, biomarker-driven design. Combined with a strengthened cash position that extends operating runway into 2027 and a valuation model that anticipates commercial launches of GPS in 2027 and SLS009 in 2029 even after applying significant risk discounts, these elements underpin his positive stance on the stock.

In another report released yesterday, Alliance Global Partners also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $10.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
